Implement this browser game as a SINGLE index.html file:

Known pitfalls learned from past games — implement to avoid these:
{known_pitfalls}
- Single index.html file with all HTML, CSS, and JS inline
- Style: html,body { margin:0; padding:0; overflow:hidden; width:100%; height:100% }
- Canvas fills viewport. Add resize listener. All coordinates relative to canvas size.
- Prevent arrow/space scrolling: window.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if(['ArrowUp','ArrowDown','ArrowLeft','ArrowRight','Space'].includes(e.code)) e.preventDefault(); });
- Click-to-start overlay for iframe keyboard focus
- Score display, win/lose state, restart on game over
- If game has continuous motion, add speed slider that multiplies velocities
- Grid-based games (snake, tetris): ALL positions (player, food, enemies) MUST be multiples of cell size. Use Math.floor(random * cols) * cellSize, never random * pixelWidth.
- Restart/reset: create a dedicated resetGame() function that resets ALL state (score, positions, arrays, gameOver flag, direction). Call it on restart.
- Collision detection: if grid-based, compare grid coords. If pixel-based, use distance or AABB. Never compare floats with ===.
- Food/item spawning: always align to the same grid as the player. Verify spawned position doesn't overlap existing entities.
Write the COMPLETE index.html with proper formatting (newlines, indentation — NOT minified, NOT a single line), then call:
Coding.tool_instruction:
[YOU MUST CALL THIS TOOL]
save_game_files_tool(files=[{"filename": "index.html", "content": "<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n..."}])
IMPORTANT: The content string MUST contain newlines (\n) and proper indentation. Do NOT output minified single-line code.
This is the ONLY way to save the game. If you don't call it, the game will not exist.

phase_prompt: You MUST ground your judgement in machine evidence, then call save_test_results_tool at the end.
Quick UI check on this game code and real browser evidence:
[UI-1] Has <!DOCTYPE html>, <canvas> or game DOM elements, and <script> tag
[UI-2] No broken HTML (unclosed tags, missing quotes)
[UI-3] Nothing permanently blocks the game area (start overlay is OK)
RULE: If the HTML is valid and the browser smoke test shows the page/canvas can render without critical console/page errors, mark PASS. Be lenient about purely visual taste.

Call save_test_results_tool(round_num={test_round}, ui_passed=<true/false>, func_passed=False, ui_issues=[<list of issues or empty>], functional_issues=[], ui_report=<short evidence-grounded report>, evidence={"html_validation": <summary>, "browser": <summary>})
You MUST ground your judgement in static JS evidence, browser smoke test evidence, and code tracing. Then call save_test_results_tool at the end.
Functional test — TRACE the code logic for this game:
For each checkpoint, TRACE the code path (don't just check if a function exists):
1. COLLISION: What exact values are compared? If grid-based, are BOTH objects on the same grid? (e.g., food.x must be a multiple of cellSize just like snake head)
2. RESTART: After game over, does clicking restart/pressing a key reset ALL state? (score=0, entities back to initial, gameOver=false, direction reset). Trace the reset function.
3. SCORING: When collision is detected, does score actually increment? Is it displayed?
4. GAME OVER: Does hitting a wall/enemy actually stop the game? Can you keep playing after losing?
Also verify each item in test_checkpoints by tracing the code.
Set functional_test_passed=true ONLY if the core game loop works: player moves, can score, can lose, can restart. Mark false if any of these are broken.

Call save_test_results_tool(round_num={test_round}, ui_passed={ui_test_passed}, func_passed=<true/false>, ui_issues={ui_issues}, functional_issues=[<issues found>], functional_report=<short evidence-grounded report>, evidence={"js_validation": <summary>, "browser": <summary>, "checkpoints": <summary>})
You MUST call save_game_files_tool at the end (unless no issues exist).
